title: Splunk ITSI KPI Degradation Alerting (per-KPI severity-tier crossings, multi-window burn-rate, density_function and predict envelope residuals, entity attribution, NEAP episode context)

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: required — KPI engine, summary indexes, NEAP, tracked alerts, adaptive thresholds

dataSources: index=itsi_summary (KPI summary events with alert_severity, alert_value, kpi_id, service identifiers); index=itsi_tracked_alerts (adaptive and static KPI severity transitions); index=itsi_grouped_alerts (Notable Event Aggregation Policy episode rollups, episode_count, MTTR hints); index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_searchlog (KPI base search skip and error telemetry); lookup itsi_kpi_inventory.csv keyed by kpi_id for service_tier, owner_team, urgency_weight, on_call_rotation, kpi_template_id; macro service_topology_lookup over itsi_summary for service_name and service_tier joins; optional MLTK trained DensityFunction models referenced from MLTK Showcase for scored anomalies
app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) plus Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit for optional density_function scoring
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation multisearch burn-rate density_function predict MTTR NEAP earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_key,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,itsi_kpi_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_title=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_title,kpi,kpi_name,"")))
      | eval entity_title=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_title,entity,entity_key,"")))
      | eval alert_severity=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_severity,severity,severity_label,"normal"))))
      | eval alert_value=tonumber(coalesce(alert_value,itsi_value,kpi_value,actual_value,null()))
      | eval current_value=coalesce(alert_value,0)
      | where len(serviceid)>2 AND len(kpi_id)>0 AND NOT match(lower(kpi_title),"servicehealthscore")
      | eval lane="kpi_summary"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id kpi_title entity_title alert_severity alert_value current_value lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,itsi_service_id,service_key,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,itsi_kpi_id,"")))
      | stats count AS tracked_sev_hits values(severity) AS tracked_sev_vals BY serviceid kpi_id
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="tracked"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id tracked_sev_hits tracked_sev_vals lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval episode_key=coalesce(episode_id,episodeid,group_id,"na")
      | stats dc(episode_key) AS episode_count min(_time) AS ep_start max(_time) AS ep_end BY serviceid
      | eval mttr_seconds=if(isnotnull(ep_start) AND isnotnull(ep_end) AND ep_end>=ep_start, ep_end-ep_start, null())
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="grouped"
      | fields _time serviceid episode_count mttr_seconds lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_key,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,itsi_kpi_id,"")))
      | eval y=tonumber(coalesce(alert_value,itsi_value,kpi_value,0))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats avg(y) AS agg5 BY _time serviceid kpi_id
      | sort 0 serviceid kpi_id + _time
      | streamstats window=6 current=t global=f avg(agg5) AS density_baseline BY serviceid kpi_id
      | eval density_function_residual=abs(agg5-density_baseline)
      | eval predict_upper=coalesce(density_baseline*1.15,agg5)
      | eval lane="density_function"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id agg5 density_baseline density_function_residual predict_upper lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_searchlog earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | where like(_raw,"%error%") OR like(_raw,"%skip%") OR like(_raw,"%fail%")
      | stats count AS kpi_base_search_fail BY host
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(host,"__kpi_base__")))
      | eval kpi_id="__kpi_engine__"
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="kpi_base_metric"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id kpi_base_search_fail lane ]
| eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,"")))
| eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,"")))
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="grouped",episode_count,null()))) AS episode_count max(eval(if(lane=="grouped",mttr_seconds,null()))) AS mttr_seconds BY serviceid
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="tracked",tracked_sev_hits,null()))) AS tracked_sev_hits BY serviceid kpi_id
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="density_function",density_function_residual,null()))) AS density_function_peak max(eval(if(lane=="density_function",predict_upper,null()))) AS predict_upper_hint BY serviceid kpi_id
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="kpi_base_metric",kpi_base_search_fail,null()))) AS kpi_base_search_fail BY serviceid
| where lane=="kpi_summary"
| bin _time span=1m
| stats latest(alert_severity) AS alert_severity latest(current_value) AS current_value latest(entity_title) AS entity_title latest(kpi_title) AS kpi_title max(density_function_peak) AS density_function_peak max(predict_upper_hint) AS predict_upper_hint max(tracked_sev_hits) AS tracked_sev_hits max(episode_count) AS episode_count max(mttr_seconds) AS mttr_seconds max(kpi_base_search_fail) AS kpi_base_search_fail BY _time serviceid kpi_id
| sort 0 serviceid kpi_id + _time
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(match(alert_severity,"critical"),1,0))) AS sev_crit_sum5 count AS cnt5 BY serviceid kpi_id
| streamstats window=30 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(match(alert_severity,"critical|high"),1,0))) AS sev_bad_sum30 count AS cnt30 BY serviceid kpi_id
| streamstats window=60 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(match(alert_severity,"critical|high|medium"),1,0))) AS sev_bad_sum60 count AS cnt60 BY serviceid kpi_id
| eval burn_5m=if(cnt5>0,sev_crit_sum5/cnt5*72,0)
| eval burn_30m=if(cnt30>0,sev_bad_sum30/cnt30*30,0)
| eval burn_60m=if(cnt60>0,sev_bad_sum60/cnt60*600,0)
| join type=left max=0 kpi_id
    [| inputlookup itsi_kpi_inventory.csv
     | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(kpi_id))
     | eval kpi_template_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_template_id,template_key,"")))
     | fields kpi_id kpi_template_id service_tier owner_team urgency_weight on_call_rotation ]
| join type=left max=0 serviceid
    [| search index=itsi_summary `service_topology_lookup`
     | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_key,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
     | dedup serviceid
     | table serviceid service_name service_tier ]
| eval entity_title=coalesce(entity_title,"unknown_entity")
| eval kpi_template_id=coalesce(kpi_template_id,"unspecified_template")
| eventstats perc50(current_value) AS baseline_p50 perc95(current_value) AS baseline_p95 stdev(current_value) AS val_stdev BY service_tier
| eval sigma_deviation=if(val_stdev>0 AND val_stdev<1000000, abs(current_value-baseline_p95)/val_stdev, 0)
| eval sigma_deviation=if(coalesce(density_function_peak,0)>0 AND sigma_deviation<coalesce(density_function_peak,0), coalesce(density_function_peak,0), sigma_deviation)
| eval severity_band=case(match(alert_severity,"critical"),"critical", match(alert_severity,"high"),"high", match(alert_severity,"medium"),"medium", true(),"low")
| eval multi_window_severity=case(severity_band=="critical" AND burn_5m>14.4 AND burn_60m>1,"critical", severity_band=="critical" OR burn_30m>4.5,"high", severity_band=="high" OR burn_60m>1,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(multi_window_severity=="critical" AND coalesce(episode_count,0)<1,"open_episode_correlation_policy_and_escalate_entity_rule_stale_risk", multi_window_severity=="critical","triage_tracked_alerts_deep_dive_kpi_base_search_clear_skips", coalesce(kpi_base_search_fail,0)>0,"repair_kpi_base_search_savedsearch_dependency_loop_and_scheduler_capacity", coalesce(tracked_sev_hits,0)>50,"review_neap_aggregation_and_correlation_rules_for_flapping", true(),"continue_kpi_threshold_adaptive_tuning_service_analyzer")
| table _time serviceid service_name service_tier kpi_id kpi_title entity_title current_value baseline_p95 sigma_deviation severity_band burn_5m burn_60m multi_window_severity recommended_action owner_team
description: Detects durable Splunk ITSI KPI degradations by correlating per-KPI alert_severity and alert_value from itsi_summary with multi-window burn-rate on one-minute bins, tracked-alert pressure, grouped episode rollups, density_function style residuals on five-minute aggregates, and KPI base search health, then enriches owner and topology context to page only on sustained tier crossings rather than transient threshold flapping.
value: Operators stop chasing single-sample KPI alarms while still catching genuine customer-impacting regressions because burn-rate and episode context separate flicker from dwell. Reliability leaders defend SLOs with evidence chains that name kpi_id, entity_title, and owner_team, while episode managers see when NEAP behavior lags raw KPI truth.
grandmaExplanation: We treat each vital business service like a bakery oven with many thermometers: one hot reading is ignored, but when the same thermometer stays too hot through short and long timers while the shop gets busier, we call the baker before the whole batch burns.
implementation: Schedule uc_13_2_2_itsi_kpi_degradation_burn_rate on the ITSI search head with earliest=-4h@h; keep itsi_summary, itsi_tracked_alerts, itsi_grouped_alerts, and itsi_searchlog forwarding; maintain itsi_kpi_inventory.csv and the service_topology_lookup macro; page on multi_window_severity critical or high with forty-five minute dedupe unless escalation occurs; route by owner_team and attach Episode Review plus Service Analyzer drilldowns.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with service reliability engineers who curate Splunk IT Service Intelligence service blueprints, the operators who keep KPI base searches scheduled on healthy search heads, and episode managers who govern Notable Event Aggregation Policy behavior. This document targets per-KPI degradation paging that survives threshold flapping, not the composite Service Health Score story that UC-13.2.1 trends across multi-window averages. You are instrumenting the KPI engine itself: alert_severity transitions, alert_value magnitudes, tracked-alert history, grouped episode rollups, and optional Machine Learning Toolkit envelopes trained in MLTK Showcase that reference density_function scoring patterns alongside classic predict-style residual bands on five-minute aggregates.

KPI design discipline is prerequisite. Keep four to six KPIs per production service unless architecture review explicitly approves denser cards. Each KPI must map to one entity rule that resolves stable entity_title values; pseudo-entities from stale rules create phantom rows that burn error budgets without customer impact. Weight KPI importance by service tier: revenue-critical parents carry higher urgency_weight in governance lookups, while internal batch services tolerate wider static bands. Document which KPIs are latency, saturation, error, or availability class so reviewers can interpret sigma_deviation consistently.

Adaptive thresholding versus static thresholds is a governance choice, not a checkbox. Adaptive policies learn seasonal shape and reduce chattering when traffic is rhythmic, yet they widen dangerously after incidents if retrain windows ingest polluted samples. Static thresholds remain appropriate for hard contractual caps such as maximum queue depth or license-adjacent limits. Pair adaptive KPIs with human-reviewed floor and ceiling guardrails, and keep a changelog entry whenever ITSI predictive analytics policies retune.

KPI Base Search architecture matters because every degradation signal ultimately depends on timely search completion. Base searches must not reference themselves through circular savedsearch chains, and dependency depth should stay shallow enough that one skipped schedule does not starve a dozen downstream KPIs. When base searches emit partial entity sets, severity can look healthy while specific entities vanish from rollups; entity rule tests and periodic sampling against raw sources close that gap.

Episode generation and correlation rules translate KPI breaches into analyst work queues. Aggregation policies decide how notables collapse into episodes, while correlation rules stitch cross-service evidence. Escalation matrices should map multi_window_severity to paging paths, chat channels, and executive bridges. Attach runbook links through notable_event enrichment so responders land on the correct remediation document instead of a generic wiki root.

Role-based access for ITSI REST endpoints such as /services/itoa_interface/service and KPI configuration URIs typically requires itoa_admin or narrowly scoped automation accounts. Store tokens in the credential store and rotate them with platform secrets policy. Read-only inventory exports may be delegated to itoa_user in some estates; never embed personal credentials in scheduled searches.

Risk briefing: multi-tenant estates share KPI templates across business units. A template edit for one brand can shift urgency_weight for another if lookups are not partitioned by tenant key. Always include tenant_id or environment in join keys when maintaining itsi_kpi_inventory.csv and topology snapshots.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.2.2 emphasizes per-KPI burn-rate across one, five, and thirty minute windows derived from summarized one-minute bins, anomaly-aware residuals inspired by density_function training workflows documented in MLTK Showcase, episode-aware context from itsi_grouped_alerts, adaptive breach history from itsi_tracked_alerts, and KPI engine stress hints from itsi_searchlog. UC-13.2.1 remains the Service Health Score regression narrative; do not merge the two alerts without explicit operational approval.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane kpi_summary reads index=itsi_summary for KPI summary events that carry alert_severity, alert_value, kpi_id, and service identifiers normalized to serviceid. Filter out Service Health Score rows at search time so this control stays sibling-distinct from UC-13.2.1. Preserve entity_title when the summary stream includes entity keys; when absent, downstream coalesce fills a sentinel so joins remain stable.

Lane tracked mines index=itsi_tracked_alerts for KPI severity transitions, adaptive threshold crossings, and continuous threshold analytics hints. Retain severity text, kpi_id, and serviceid. Flatten multivalue fields at ingest when possible so SPL does not rely on fragile spath at runtime.

Lane grouped reads index=itsi_grouped_alerts for episode rollups. Capture episode identifiers, minimum and maximum event times for MTTR style math, and serviceid keys that align with summary indexes. If episode identifiers are nested, flatten them in Heavy Forwarder parsing to keep dc(episode_key) honest.

Lane density_function materializes five-minute averages of alert_value per kpi_id and serviceid, then applies streamstats to form a short-horizon baseline and residual magnitude. In full MLTK deployments, replace the inline residual with a trained DensityFunction model applied through MLTK Showcases; the comment macro in the SPL reminds operators where to wire training jobs without blocking alert delivery when ML workspaces are offline.

Lane kpi_base_metric watches index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_searchlog for skip, error, and failure language tied to KPI scheduling. Map host to a synthetic serviceid bucket when service-specific identifiers are unavailable so scheduler pain still surfaces.

Maintain lookup table itsi_kpi_inventory.csv keyed by kpi_id with columns for kpi_template_id, service_tier, owner_team, urgency_weight, and on_call_rotation. Refresh from configuration management after content-pack imports. Maintain macro service_topology_lookup that expands to a safe filter identifying topology export events in itsi_summary; the join subsearch deduplicates serviceid to service_name and service_tier for attribution.

props.conf and transforms.conf should preserve precise _time for itsi_summary and alert indexes. Retain at least four hours of hot data on search heads for the multisearch window and longer on indexers for investigations. Legal reviewers may require masking entity_title when hostnames embed customer names.

Optional HEC mirrors for ITSI REST inventory snapshots can land JSON for service trees; keep sourcetype discipline so topology macros remain inexpensive.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_2_itsi_kpi_degradation_burn_rate with a five-minute or one-minute schedule depending on organizational noise tolerance, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when multi_window_severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate serviceid and kpi_id pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies and attach Episode Review deep links plus Service Analyzer KPI drilldowns.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five lanes so a single silent index cannot hide degradation. eventstats lifts episode_count, mttr_seconds, tracked_sev_hits, density_function_peak, predict_upper_hint, and kpi_base_search_fail onto KPI summary rows before per-minute binning. streamstats implements sliding windows of five, thirty, and sixty samples to approximate five-minute, thirty-minute, and sixty-minute burn-rate behavior without expensive time_window joins on irregular events. join enriches kpi governance fields from itsi_kpi_inventory.csv and service names from the topology macro subsearch. case maps alert_severity text into severity_band, then multi_window_severity encodes the paging ladder with the critical gate severity_band=critical AND burn_5m>14.4 AND burn_60m>1.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation multisearch burn-rate density_function predict MTTR NEAP earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_key,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,itsi_kpi_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_title=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_title,kpi,kpi_name,"")))
      | eval entity_title=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_title,entity,entity_key,"")))
      | eval alert_severity=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_severity,severity,severity_label,"normal"))))
      | eval alert_value=tonumber(coalesce(alert_value,itsi_value,kpi_value,actual_value,null()))
      | eval current_value=coalesce(alert_value,0)
      | where len(serviceid)>2 AND len(kpi_id)>0 AND NOT match(lower(kpi_title),"servicehealthscore")
      | eval lane="kpi_summary"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id kpi_title entity_title alert_severity alert_value current_value lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,itsi_service_id,service_key,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,itsi_kpi_id,"")))
      | stats count AS tracked_sev_hits values(severity) AS tracked_sev_vals BY serviceid kpi_id
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="tracked"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id tracked_sev_hits tracked_sev_vals lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval episode_key=coalesce(episode_id,episodeid,group_id,"na")
      | stats dc(episode_key) AS episode_count min(_time) AS ep_start max(_time) AS ep_end BY serviceid
      | eval mttr_seconds=if(isnotnull(ep_start) AND isnotnull(ep_end) AND ep_end>=ep_start, ep_end-ep_start, null())
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="grouped"
      | fields _time serviceid episode_count mttr_seconds lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_key,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,itsi_kpi_id,"")))
      | eval y=tonumber(coalesce(alert_value,itsi_value,kpi_value,0))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats avg(y) AS agg5 BY _time serviceid kpi_id
      | sort 0 serviceid kpi_id + _time
      | streamstats window=6 current=t global=f avg(agg5) AS density_baseline BY serviceid kpi_id
      | eval density_function_residual=abs(agg5-density_baseline)
      | eval predict_upper=coalesce(density_baseline*1.15,agg5)
      | eval lane="density_function"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id agg5 density_baseline density_function_residual predict_upper lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_searchlog earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | where like(_raw,"%error%") OR like(_raw,"%skip%") OR like(_raw,"%fail%")
      | stats count AS kpi_base_search_fail BY host
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(host,"__kpi_base__")))
      | eval kpi_id="__kpi_engine__"
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="kpi_base_metric"
      | fields _time serviceid kpi_id kpi_base_search_fail lane ]
| eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,"")))
| eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,"")))
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="grouped",episode_count,null()))) AS episode_count max(eval(if(lane=="grouped",mttr_seconds,null()))) AS mttr_seconds BY serviceid
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="tracked",tracked_sev_hits,null()))) AS tracked_sev_hits BY serviceid kpi_id
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="density_function",density_function_residual,null()))) AS density_function_peak max(eval(if(lane=="density_function",predict_upper,null()))) AS predict_upper_hint BY serviceid kpi_id
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="kpi_base_metric",kpi_base_search_fail,null()))) AS kpi_base_search_fail BY serviceid
| where lane=="kpi_summary"
| bin _time span=1m
| stats latest(alert_severity) AS alert_severity latest(current_value) AS current_value latest(entity_title) AS entity_title latest(kpi_title) AS kpi_title max(density_function_peak) AS density_function_peak max(predict_upper_hint) AS predict_upper_hint max(tracked_sev_hits) AS tracked_sev_hits max(episode_count) AS episode_count max(mttr_seconds) AS mttr_seconds max(kpi_base_search_fail) AS kpi_base_search_fail BY _time serviceid kpi_id
| sort 0 serviceid kpi_id + _time
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(match(alert_severity,"critical"),1,0))) AS sev_crit_sum5 count AS cnt5 BY serviceid kpi_id
| streamstats window=30 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(match(alert_severity,"critical|high"),1,0))) AS sev_bad_sum30 count AS cnt30 BY serviceid kpi_id
| streamstats window=60 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(match(alert_severity,"critical|high|medium"),1,0))) AS sev_bad_sum60 count AS cnt60 BY serviceid kpi_id
| eval burn_5m=if(cnt5>0,sev_crit_sum5/cnt5*72,0)
| eval burn_30m=if(cnt30>0,sev_bad_sum30/cnt30*30,0)
| eval burn_60m=if(cnt60>0,sev_bad_sum60/cnt60*600,0)
| join type=left max=0 kpi_id
    [| inputlookup itsi_kpi_inventory.csv
     | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(kpi_id))
     | eval kpi_template_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_template_id,template_key,"")))
     | fields kpi_id kpi_template_id service_tier owner_team urgency_weight on_call_rotation ]
| join type=left max=0 serviceid
    [| search index=itsi_summary `service_topology_lookup`
     | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_key,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
     | dedup serviceid
     | table serviceid service_name service_tier ]
| eval entity_title=coalesce(entity_title,"unknown_entity")
| eval kpi_template_id=coalesce(kpi_template_id,"unspecified_template")
| eventstats perc50(current_value) AS baseline_p50 perc95(current_value) AS baseline_p95 stdev(current_value) AS val_stdev BY service_tier
| eval sigma_deviation=if(val_stdev>0 AND val_stdev<1000000, abs(current_value-baseline_p95)/val_stdev, 0)
| eval sigma_deviation=if(coalesce(density_function_peak,0)>0 AND sigma_deviation<coalesce(density_function_peak,0), coalesce(density_function_peak,0), sigma_deviation)
| eval severity_band=case(match(alert_severity,"critical"),"critical", match(alert_severity,"high"),"high", match(alert_severity,"medium"),"medium", true(),"low")
| eval multi_window_severity=case(severity_band=="critical" AND burn_5m>14.4 AND burn_60m>1,"critical", severity_band=="critical" OR burn_30m>4.5,"high", severity_band=="high" OR burn_60m>1,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(multi_window_severity=="critical" AND coalesce(episode_count,0)<1,"open_episode_correlation_policy_and_escalate_entity_rule_stale_risk", multi_window_severity=="critical","triage_tracked_alerts_deep_dive_kpi_base_search_clear_skips", coalesce(kpi_base_search_fail,0)>0,"repair_kpi_base_search_savedsearch_dependency_loop_and_scheduler_capacity", coalesce(tracked_sev_hits,0)>50,"review_neap_aggregation_and_correlation_rules_for_flapping", true(),"continue_kpi_threshold_adaptive_tuning_service_analyzer")
| table _time serviceid service_name service_tier kpi_id kpi_title entity_title current_value baseline_p95 sigma_deviation severity_band burn_5m burn_60m multi_window_severity recommended_action owner_team
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_2_itsi_kpi_degradation_burn_rate_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_2_itsi_kpi_degradation_burn_rate | where multi_window_severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time beyond internal SLOs, materialize each lane into a summary index keyed by serviceid, kpi_id, and minute bucket, then widen operational alerts to read summaries while retaining this search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Begin in a lab service with non-production KPI thresholds. Induce a deliberate severity escalation on a single KPI while holding others stable, then confirm alert_value and alert_severity move in itsi_summary within the same minute bins that Service Analyzer shows for the KPI deep dive. Capture screenshots tied to the Splunk row identifiers in one ticket.

Validate burn-rate gating by injecting brief spikes that recover within two minutes; critical multi_window_severity should stay quiet if burn_60m remains below the durable threshold. Extend the degradation for fifteen minutes and confirm the critical ladder engages with synchronized rises in burn_5m and burn_60m.

Validate inventory joins by running inputlookup itsi_kpi_inventory.csv alone and confirming kpi_id coverage for tier-zero paths. Join misses should surface unrated service_tier and unowned owner_team, which is useful for governance backlogs but unacceptable for payment flows.

Validate topology joins by comparing service_name from the macro subsearch to REST exports from ITSI service inventory. If titles disagree, refresh topology snapshots before tuning urgency_weight.

Validate MLTK alignment by training a DensityFunction model on historical alert_value for a canary kpi_id in MLTK Showcase, then comparing model scores to density_function_residual magnitudes in this search for a retrospective window. Predict-oriented baselines should move together unless retrain windows were polluted.

Negative validation: after recovery, when alert_severity returns to normal for sixty minutes, filtering multi_window_severity to critical or high should return zero rows for the canary KPI.

Synthetic episode validation: force related notables that should group, confirm episode_count rises in itsi_grouped_alerts, and verify recommended_action shifts when episodes are absent during critical KPI breaches.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Stale entity rules causing pseudo-entity drift: KPI rows show rotating entity_title values while infrastructure is unchanged; freeze template edits, replay entity discovery, and rebind rules before trusting sigma_deviation.

Case 2 — ITSI scheduler skew during search head failover: itsi_searchlog shows clustered skips; shift workloads, verify captain stability, and delay paging until burn signals persist independent of skip bursts.

Case 3 — KPI base search saved-search dependency loop: schedules stall with circular references; break the loop, split base searches, and reauthorize tokens before reopening episodes.

Case 4 — Mismatched timezone in collection: minute bins misalign to business hours; fix TZ on forwarders and search heads, then replay validation windows.

Case 5 — Anomaly model retraining window pollution: adaptive thresholds widen after incidents; annotate retrain periods, tighten guardrails, and compare MLTK Showcase exports to density_function_peak outliers.

Case 6 — Multi-tenant noise from shared KPI templates: urgency_weight from itsi_kpi_inventory.csv routes pages to the wrong on_call_rotation; partition lookups by tenant and reimport content packs with scoped keys.

Case 7 — Wrong urgency_weight after content-pack upgrade: governance CSV stale; rerun configuration management, diff kpi_template_id, and reconcile escalation matrix entries.

Case 8 — Missing data yields severity unknown versus critical: null alert_value rows appear; decide explicit unknown policy, gate pages on fillnull strategy, and open data-quality tickets for silent entities.

Case 9 — SOC drilldown breaking due to deprecated drilldown URL: notable_event enrichment links 404; update runbook attachments and REST-driven URL templates in correlation actions.

Case 10 — Batch backfill double-counting: replay jobs duplicate summary rows; dedupe by source and _time in upstream transforms before trusting burn_60m.

Case 11 — Unhealthy KPI rollup hidden by service health average: composite scores look fine while one KPI burns; keep this per-KPI alert enabled even when executive tiles stay green.

Case 12 — Correlation rule storms duplicate notables: episode_count explodes without added insight; tune NEAP similarity thresholds and break glass only after verifying KPI signal quality.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists sixteen columns including service_name and owner_team.
visualization: Service Analyzer KPI deep links, Episode Review episode tables with MTTR, heatmaps of burn_5m versus burn_60m, sigma_deviation sparklines, owner_team routing tiles, and MLTK Showcase density score overlays for retrospective review.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS entity_host
| join type=left max=0 entity_host [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.signature) AS alert_sig latest(Alerts.severity) AS alert_sev FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS entity_host ]
| join type=left max=0 entity_host [| inputlookup itsi_kpi_inventory.csv | rename kpi_entity_host AS entity_host | fields entity_host owner_team service_tier ]
| table entity_host cpu_load mem_used alert_sig alert_sev owner_team service_tier
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for hosts referenced in ITSI entity translate rules; accelerate Alerts summaries when ITSI notables or correlation searches map into Alerts.dest for cross-domain drilldowns; maintain Inventory-aligned lookups for service tier joins used in governance routing.

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate end-of-day batch jobs can depress latency KPIs for twenty minutes without customer impact; tag those windows in change metadata before interpreting burn_60m. ITSI version upgrades sometimes reset adaptive threshold baselines and can flash high severity for an hour; compare release notes and freeze paging until retrain completes. Content-pack import cycles relink templates and may duplicate kpi_id rows until lookups refresh; dedupe inventory before blaming applications. Quarterly on-call drills intentionally breach sandbox KPIs; exclude drill environments via service_tier filters. Planned maintenance windows suppress episodes but not raw summary indexes; cross-check maintenance tickets before treating missing episode_count as governance failure. Synthetic monitoring accounts can drive alert_value spikes when scripts retry aggressively; segregate synthetic entities in entity rules. Clock skew between search heads and indexers misaligns minute bins; repair time synchronization before tuning burn constants. Shared service templates across regions can route urgency_weight incorrectly when lookups lack region keys; partition CSV rows. Backfill replays after indexer recovery may stack duplicate alert_severity transitions; use deduped source keys upstream. Executive rehearse days can inflate tracked_sev_hits without production risk; annotate rehearsal labels in correlation rules.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads running ITSI with indexer clusters retaining itsi_summary and alert indexes, typically on Linux or Kubernetes-hosted Splunk containers.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.1 Service Health Score multi-window regression detection, UC-13.2.10 KPI base search skip-only surveillance in isolation, generic Enterprise Security correlation except where NEAP objects are intentionally shared, or greenfield ITSI installation procedures.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab ITSI tenant, hold one KPI in critical alert_severity for fifteen minutes while alert_value remains abnormal, confirm itsi_summary rows accumulate, burn_5m and burn_60m cross configured gates, and uc_13_2_2_itsi_kpi_degradation_burn_rate returns multi_window_severity critical with non-null owner_team within two scheduled runs.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring the KPI to normal severity for sixty minutes with quiet itsi_searchlog skip bursts and stable episodes, filtering multi_window_severity to critical or high should return zero rows for the canary kpi_id.

evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_2_itsi_kpi_degradation_burn_rate with schedule and throttle metadata, exported CSV of alert rows joined to itsi_kpi_inventory.csv, and ticketed screenshots from Service Analyzer KPI view and Episode Review for the same serviceid.
